description: OpenShock Product-Grade Iteration - stabilize the local Phase 0 baseline, align product truth, reduce first-run friction, harden request-scoped auth, and add verifiable memory/runtime/release evidence.

nextRoadmap.title: OpenShock Agent OS Roadmap
priorityOrder: M2 Agent Runner And Long Sessions
calibration: OpenShock already has agent profiles, mailbox/handoff, governance, runtime registry, memory center, release gate, headed evidence, and fresh workspace agents.
The next gap is connecting existing primitives through a continuous scheduling kernel, not rebuilding them from scratch.
Existing agents are identities and handoff participants, but not yet always-on, wakeable, long-running teammates.
WORKFLOW.md should be runtime-readable hot-reload policy for polling, concurrency, Definition of Done, hooks, and agent prompt policy.
Agent Done should require evidence and release gate truth, not only model text.

userStories.title: Stabilize fresh stack and local verification baseline
userStories.description: As a local OpenShock operator, I want the fresh stack and verification commands to run reliably on macOS so that every later product iteration starts from a green baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/web dev startup uses the same stable Next.js dev mode as the fresh-stack script
scripts/dev-fresh-stack-contract.test.mjs asserts the selected Next.js dev mode
pnpm test:web-contracts runs TypeScript contract tests without unsupported Node flags
Auth recovery actions that establish an active session return a token and set the request auth cookie
pnpm verify passes with the nvm Node 20 path documented in progress notes when needed
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-04-28. Verified web and fresh-stack dev paths both use next dev --webpack; fresh-stack contract covers the dev mode; test:web-contracts runs through node --import tsx; recovery actions refresh request auth token/cookie. Evidence: node --test scripts/dev-fresh-stack-contract.test.mjs, pnpm test:web-contracts, cd apps/server && ../../scripts/go.sh test ./internal/api -run TestRecoveryChallengeRoutesConsumeVerifyAndAuthorizeOnce -count=1, and PATH=/Users/lark/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.2/bin:$PATH pnpm verify all passed.

userStories.title: Align product TODO and checklist truth
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want TODO, Checklist, and Runbook wording to agree on current auth, release evidence, and fresh-stack status so that I do not have to reconcile contradictory project truth.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/product/TODO-List.md lists only active product gaps and removes completed P0 sections from current priority
docs/product/Checklist.md no longer claims no-token requests fall back to global Auth.Session when request auth is fail-closed
docs/engineering/Runbook.md documents the macOS Node 20 path workaround only as an environment note, not as a product dependency
docs/testing/README.md points reviewers to generated evidence commands instead of stale dates
git diff --check passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-04-28. TODO current priority now lists only active gaps, completed auth challenge/release evidence/headed suite sections were removed from current priority, Checklist no longer claims no-token fallback to global Auth.Session, Runbook documents the macOS Node 20 PATH workaround as an environment note, and Testing Index tells reviewers to regenerate missing evidence rather than trust stale dates. Evidence: rg stale auth/evidence phrases returned no matches, git diff --check passed, and PATH=/Users/lark/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.2/bin:$PATH pnpm typecheck:web passed.

userStories.title: Add local walkthrough evidence locator
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want local route and interaction smoke evidence to be discoverable from one command so that local walkthroughs do not become invisible untracked artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a script that prints the latest output/testing/local-walkthrough route smoke, interaction smoke, and screenshot paths
The script exits non-zero when route-smoke.json or interaction-smoke.json is missing
Add a node --test contract for the evidence locator script
package.json exposes the evidence locator command
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-04-28. Added scripts/local-walkthrough-evidence-latest.mjs to print output/testing/local-walkthrough route smoke, interaction smoke, screenshot paths, and fail when required smoke JSON is missing. Added scripts/local-walkthrough-evidence-latest.test.mjs and package script local-walkthrough:evidence. Evidence: node --test scripts/local-walkthrough-evidence-latest.test.mjs, pnpm local-walkthrough:evidence, git diff --check, and PATH=/Users/lark/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.2/bin:$PATH pnpm typecheck:web all passed.

userStories.title: Unify home access and setup first-start targets
userStories.description: As a first-time user, I want home, access, and setup to point to the same next step so that the product has one obvious first successful path.
acceptanceCriteria: home-first-screen, access-first-screen, setup-first-screen, and critical-loop contracts all assert the same first-start next route for a fresh workspace
The visible home primary action and access recovery next action resolve through the shared first-start journey helper
/onboarding remains a compatibility route and is not presented as a competing primary path
pnpm test:web-contracts passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed on 2026-04-28: pnpm test:web-contracts (116 tests), headed fresh critical loop with real owner/runtime/agent state at output/testing/us-004-first-start/critical-loop.md, Go sanitize/bootstrap targeted tests, pnpm typecheck:web, and git diff --check. The headed harness now verifies created issue/room recovery through the authenticated browser control-state API, matching request-scoped auth.

userStories.title: Reduce Board planning mirror duplication
userStories.description: As a user creating work from Board, I want the Board page to show only planning status and the shortest route back to discussion so that it stays a supporting flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Board first screen keeps one primary create action and one discussion return action
Board cards do not repeat the same room next-step summary already shown in Rooms or Room detail
apps/web/src/lib/board-inbox-first-screen.test.ts covers the reduced Board copy contract
pnpm test:web-contracts passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed on 2026-04-28: Board cards no longer render card.summary; they keep title, planning state, owner, source, and one 回讨论间 action. board-inbox-first-screen.test.ts covers the compact Board card contract, pnpm test:web-contracts passed with 117 tests, headed browser evidence passed at output/testing/us-005-board-planning/report.md, pnpm typecheck:web passed after regenerating .next/dev/types, and git diff --check passed.

userStories.title: Reduce Rooms supporting summary duplication
userStories.description: As a user returning to work, I want Rooms to show the single best continue target without repeating the same next-step summary in multiple cards.
acceptanceCriteria: /rooms first screen renders one continue card with one primary CTA when a continue target exists
The Rooms snapshot area does not duplicate the continue card summary text
apps/web/src/lib/rooms-first-screen.test.ts asserts stable continue anchors and no duplicate summary fallback
pnpm test:web-contracts passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed on 2026-04-28: /rooms keeps one continue card with one primary CTA, RoomSnapshotCard no longer renders room.summary or room.topic.summary, and snapshot cards keep compact topic/owner/run/unread facts plus one always-visible 进入讨论间 action. rooms-first-screen.test.ts covers stable continue/snapshot anchors and no duplicate summary fallback. Headed browser evidence passed after logging in with request-scoped seed owner auth at output/testing/us-006-rooms-summary/report.md; screenshots are under output/testing/us-006-rooms-summary/run-attempt-2/screenshots/. Verification passed with PATH=/Users/lark/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.2/bin:$PATH node --import tsx --test apps/web/src/lib/rooms-first-screen.test.ts, pnpm test:web-contracts (118 tests), OPENSHOCK_E2E_ARTIFACTS_DIR=output/testing/us-006-rooms-summary/run-attempt-2 pnpm test:headed-rooms-continue-entry -- --report output/testing/us-006-rooms-summary/report.md, pnpm typecheck:web, and git diff --check.

userStories.title: Reduce Settings supporting flow duplication
userStories.description: As a workspace owner, I want Settings to surface default entry, member truth, and machine status before advanced panels so that routine setup does not feel like an admin maze.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings first screen keeps workspace/member/machine defaults above advanced governance, credentials, and notification panels
Advanced settings link is present but does not duplicate the same summary cards on the main Settings page
apps/web/src/lib/settings-first-screen.test.ts covers the reduced main Settings hierarchy

userStories.title: Close request-scoped auth mutation response tail items
userStories.description: As a multi-user workspace member, I want permission-gated mutation responses to return my request-scoped session so that another user's last login never leaks into my response state.
acceptanceCriteria: Add or extend API contract coverage for at least two remaining permission-gated mutation responses that return state
The covered responses set state.Auth.Session from the request token or cookie caller
Signed-out requests for the covered mutations return 401 or 403 without a full state side channel
cd apps/server && ../../scripts/go.sh test ./internal/api -run RequestScoped -count=1 passes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed on 2026-04-28: run control and run sandbox mutation responses now return sanitized request-scoped state, and RequestScoped contract coverage proves member browser requests keep mina@openshock.dev even after an owner browser login. Signed-out run control and sandbox mutations return auth failures without a state payload. Verification passed with env GOCACHE=/private/tmp/openshock-go-build-cache ../../scripts/go.sh test ./internal/api -run RequestScoped -count=1, env GOCACHE=/private/tmp/openshock-go-build-cache ../../scripts/go.sh test ./internal/api -run 'RequestScoped|RunControl|SandboxPolicy' -count=1, PATH=/Users/lark/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.2/bin:$PATH pnpm typecheck:web, jq empty ralph/prd.json, and git diff --check. Broader ./internal/api -count=1 hit an existing 10m timeout in TestAutoCompleteDeliveryDelegationKeepsBlockedRuntimeRoomHotButMarksRouteDoneInGovernanceSnapshot while waiting on mailbox auto-followup daemon exec; no US-008 failure was observed before the timeout.

userStories.title: Add auth recovery browser evidence for token refresh
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want headed or browser-level evidence that verify email, authorize device, and bind external identity refresh request auth so that recovery does not silently leave stale cookies.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a browser smoke or headed script that logs in, consumes verify_email, consumes authorize_device, and confirms subsequent /v1/auth/session reflects the recovered caller
The script writes a JSON report under output/testing or docs/testing/artifacts
The report records failed responses, console problems, and final session email/device status
package.json exposes the script command
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed on 2026-04-28: added scripts/headed-auth-recovery-refresh.mjs and package command test:headed-auth-recovery-refresh. The headed browser smoke starts isolated server/web services, logs in an owner, invites a reviewer, logs the reviewer in, consumes verify_email and authorize_device challenges through browser fetch with credentials, then confirms /v1/auth/session still reflects the recovered reviewer with verified email and authorized device. JSON evidence is output/testing/us-009-auth-recovery-refresh/report.json with failedResponses=[], consoleProblems=[], and finalSession recorded. Verification passed with node --check scripts/headed-auth-recovery-refresh.mjs, OPENSHOCK_CHROMIUM_PATH='/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome' OPENSHOCK_E2E_ARTIFACTS_DIR=output/testing/us-009-auth-recovery-refresh/run-attempt pnpm test:headed-auth-recovery-refresh -- --report output/testing/us-009-auth-recovery-refresh/report.json, pnpm test:web-contracts (119 tests), pnpm typecheck:web, jq empty ralph/prd.json, and git diff --check.

userStories.title: Add memory cleanup dry-run contract
userStories.description: As a workspace operator, I want memory cleanup to expose a dry-run result before destructive cleanup so that retention automation is reviewable and safe.
acceptanceCriteria: Memory cleanup API supports a dry-run or preview mode that returns due items without mutating durable memory state
The dry-run response includes due count, nextRunAt, and item identifiers or summaries sufficient for review
Add store and API tests proving dry-run does not change memory-center durable state
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed on 2026-04-28: added memory cleanup dry-run/preview support through store PreviewMemoryCleanup and POST /v1/memory-center/cleanup?mode=dry-run or mode=preview. The response includes dryRun/executed flags, dueCount, nextRunAt, due item identifiers, source summaries, reasons, preview stats, center, and request-scoped state without mutating durable memory-center promotions or cleanup ledger. Store and API tests prove dry-run leaves memory-center.json, promotions, and cleanup ledger unchanged across reload. Verification passed with env GOCACHE=/private/tmp/openshock-go-build-cache ../../scripts/go.sh test ./internal/store -run MemoryCleanup -count=1, env GOCACHE=/private/tmp/openshock-go-build-cache ../../scripts/go.sh test ./internal/api -run MemoryCenterCleanup -count=1, PATH=/Users/lark/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.2/bin:$PATH pnpm typecheck:web, jq empty ralph/prd.json, and git diff --check.

userStories.title: Add memory compaction queue truth
userStories.description: As a memory reviewer, I want compaction candidates to be visible as a queue so that long-running memory cleanup can be governed before it writes back.
acceptanceCriteria: Memory center state includes a compaction queue with candidate id, source artifact id, reason, status, and updatedAt
Add store helpers to enqueue, list, approve, and dismiss compaction candidates
Add tests proving queue entries persist across store reload
No UI changes are required in this story
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed on 2026-04-28: memory-center durable state now includes compactionQueue entries with id, sourceArtifactId, sourcePath/sourceSummary, reason, status, createdAt, updatedAt, and updatedBy. Added store helpers MemoryCompactionQueue, EnqueueMemoryCompactionCandidate, ApproveMemoryCompactionCandidate, and DismissMemoryCompactionCandidate. Added store coverage proving queued, approved, and dismissed compaction candidates persist across memory-center.json reloads. No UI changes were made. Verification passed with env GOCACHE=/private/tmp/openshock-go-build-cache ../../scripts/go.sh test ./internal/store -run MemoryCompaction -count=1, env GOCACHE=/private/tmp/openshock-go-build-cache ../../scripts/go.sh test ./internal/store -run 'MemoryCleanup|MemoryCompaction' -count=1, PATH=/Users/lark/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.2/bin:$PATH pnpm typecheck:web, jq empty ralph/prd.json, and git diff --check.

acceptanceCriteria: /memory renders compaction candidates from memory-center state in a collapsed supporting section
Each candidate shows reason, source artifact, status, and approve or dismiss actions
The Memory UI does not place compaction above the default file stack on first screen
Add web contract coverage for the compaction queue anchors

userStories.title: Add fake external memory provider contract
userStories.description: As a platform integrator, I want a deterministic fake external memory provider contract so that OpenShock can test external durable fallback without claiming a real provider integration.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an internal fake external provider adapter used only by tests or local harnesses
The fake adapter can simulate healthy, degraded, and recovery states
Memory provider check and recover tests cover fake external provider degraded fallback and recovery
The UI-visible provider status continues to label real external persistent mode as not configured unless real config exists
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Passed on 2026-04-29: added a deterministic file-backed fake external memory provider adapter at .openshock/memory/external-persistent/fake-adapter.json for tests/local harnesses only. The fake adapter can report degraded or healthy and recovery transitions degraded fake state to healthy, while real external-persistent without a real config remains degraded/not configured even after local relay scaffold recovery. Memory provider check/recover coverage now exercises fake degraded fallback and recovery, and the Memory UI status label renders unconfigured real external persistent state as 未配置 instead of claiming a production integration. Headed browser evidence passed at output/testing/us-013-fake-external-provider/report.md with screenshots under output/testing/us-013-fake-external-provider/run-attempt/run/screenshots/. Verification passed with env GOCACHE=/private/tmp/openshock-go-build-cache ../../scripts/go.sh test ./internal/store -run 'MemoryProvider|MemoryCenterPreviewPrefersCurrentOwner' -count=1, env GOCACHE=/private/tmp/openshock-go-build-cache ../../scripts/go.sh test ./internal/api -run 'MemoryCenterProvider|MemoryCenterRecoveryMatrix' -count=1, PATH=/Users/lark/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.2/bin:$PATH node --check scripts/headed-memory-provider-orchestration.mjs, PATH=/Users/lark/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.2/bin:$PATH node --import tsx --test apps/web/src/lib/memory-provider-ui.test.ts apps/web/src/lib/memory-compaction-ui.test.ts, PATH=/Users/lark/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.2/bin:$PATH pnpm test:web-contracts (123 tests), PATH=/Users/lark/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.2/bin:$PATH OPENSHOCK_CHROMIUM_PATH='/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome' OPENSHOCK_E2E_ARTIFACTS_DIR=output/testing/us-013-fake-external-provider/run-attempt pnpm test:headed-memory-provider-orchestration -- --report output/testing/us-013-fake-external-provider/report.md, and PATH=/Users/lark/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.2/bin:$PATH pnpm typecheck:web.

userStories.title: Add runtime readiness hook contract
userStories.description: As a runtime operator, I want scheduler decisions to include readiness reasons so that failover is based on explicit runtime health rather than hidden heuristics.
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime registry exposes a readiness status and readiness reason for each runtime
Scheduler decisions include the selected runtime readiness reason
Add server tests for offline, stale, busy, and ready runtime selection cases
Setup or Agents surfaces display the readiness reason without adding a new primary action
Tests pass

userStories.title: Fail release evidence lookup when latest artifacts are missing
userStories.description: As a release reviewer, I want evidence lookup to fail clearly when the latest report points to missing artifacts so that stale release evidence cannot look green.
acceptanceCriteria: release:evidence:latest exits non-zero when the latest RC or full report points to a missing artifacts directory
The command prints the exact rerun command for the missing evidence type
Add node --test coverage for missing RC artifacts and missing full evidence
docs/testing/README.md mentions that missing artifacts are release-blocking for reviewer evidence
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run product-grade release verification sweep
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want one final verification story that regenerates current evidence after the iteration so that the product-grade loop closes with auditable proof.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm verify passes
Local walkthrough route smoke and interaction smoke pass and produce JSON evidence
pnpm verify:release:full passes or progress notes record the exact blocker and failing command
pnpm release:evidence:latest full resolves to an existing report and artifacts directory when full gate passes
ralph/progress.txt summarizes completed stories and remaining blockers
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
